en.extract:
The grey-capped hemispingus (Kleinothraupis reyi) is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae that is endemic to Venezuela.
Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests where it is threatened by habitat loss.


Description
The species has a grey crown and does not have any supercilium. Upperparts are olive in colour and underparts are yellow.


Habitat
It is found on elevations between 2,200 metres (7,200 ft) and 3,000 metres (9,800 ft).


References

The '''grey-capped hemispingus''' ('''''Kleinothraupis reyi''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Tanager|Thraupidae]] that is [[endemic]] to [[Venezuela]].

Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s where it is threatened by [[habitat destruction|habitat loss]].

==Description==
The species has a grey crown and does not have any [[supercilium]]. Upperparts are olive in colour and underparts are yellow.<ref name=neo>{{cite web|url=https://neotropical.birds.cornell.edu/portal/species/overview?p_p_spp=584716|title=''Hemispingus reyi''|publisher=[[Neotropical Birds Online]]|access-date=2 June 2013}}</ref>

==Habitat==
It is found on elevations between {{convert|2,200|m}} and {{convert|3,000|m}}.<ref name=neo/>
